setting-up-environment.label: Setting Up the Environment
setting-up-environment.summary: Guide to installing Python and setting up the IDE.

python-installation-quiz.prompt: To ensure Python is correctly installed, you should check if it is added to your _____.

python-installation-check.title: Python Installation Check
python-installation-check.prompt: What should you do after downloading Python?
python-installation-check.hint: Make sure to configure your installation.

help.concept: After downloading Python, you need to run the installer and follow the setup instructions.
help.hint_1: Look for an option to add Python to your system PATH.
help.hint_2: This step is crucial for running Python from the command line.

options.a: Run the installer and follow the instructions
options.b: Open Python directly from the download folder
options.c: Ignore the installer and use a different version

ide-choice-quiz.title: Choosing an IDE
ide-choice-quiz.prompt: Which of the following are popular IDEs for Python? Select all that apply.
ide-choice-quiz.hint: Look for environments specifically designed for Python development.

help.concept: Popular IDEs for Python include those that provide features like code completion and debugging.
help.hint_1: One of them is known for its powerful features and is developed by JetBrains.
help.hint_2: Another is a simple text editor that supports Python syntax highlighting.

python-ide-setup.title: Create a Simple Python Script
python-ide-setup.prompt: Create a Python script that prints 'Hello, World!' to the console.
python-ide-setup.hint: Use a print statement with the exact text from the prompt.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can create a simple Python script that prints text.
help.hint_1: Write the required Python steps first, then run the program with the sample input.
help.hint_2: Make sure your final print output matches the prompt exactly.

intro-to-python-installation.title: Installing Python
intro-to-python-installation.bodyMarkdown:
To start coding in Python, you first need to install it on your computer. Visit the official [Python website](https://www.python.org/downloads/) and download the latest version suitable for your operating system. Follow the installation instructions provided on the website. 

**Example:** After downloading, run the installer and ensure to check the box that says 'Add Python to PATH'. This will make it easier to run Python from the command line.

**Your turn:** Try installing Python on your machine!

setting-up-ide.title: Setting Up Your IDE
setting-up-ide.bodyMarkdown:
An Integrated Development Environment (IDE) helps you write and manage your code efficiently. A popular choice for Python is [PyCharm](https://www.jetbrains.com/pycharm/). Download and install it, then create a new project. 

**Example:** In PyCharm, you can create a new project by selecting 'New Project' from the welcome screen. Choose a location and ensure the interpreter is set to the Python version you installed.

**Try this:** Open PyCharm and create a new project!
